objections.objection_text: I can't be with her because she's past her prime and already has a child with another man.
objections.underlying_belief: My legacy depends on biological 'firsts' and purity of the family line rather than God's plan.
objections.underlying_fear: I am being cheated out of the 'ideal' romance fantasy; my ego won't survive 'seconds'.
objections.counter_truth_angle: God uses messy, non-linear families throughout scripture to establish His kingdom; holiness isn't a straight line.
objections.transcript_proof_quote_redacted: It'd be annoying to not get to fulfill my fantasy of having a firstborn... my woman's been with another man and given him another kid, and I'm just kind of like getting the seconds

objections.objection_text: I'm afraid a relationship will ruin my morning routine and my spiritual focus.
objections.underlying_belief: God is only accessible in silence and isolation; a woman is an 'interruption' to holiness.
objections.underlying_fear: Loss of control; I can't maintain my discipline if someone else triggers my lust or chaos.
objections.counter_truth_angle: A wife isn't an interruption to the mission; she IS the mission or the primary helper to it. True discipline holds in the presence of others.
objections.transcript_proof_quote_redacted: When I'm by myself, I love kind of like having my morning routine... The second she comes into my world, that obviously gets interrupted. And I almost resent her for that.
